name: Lauren Reinhold
occupation: Attorney and former Social Security Administration employee
education: J.D., University of Kansas School of Law
district: Kansas 1st Congressional District (the "Big First") — covers 60+ counties spanning over half the state, including Manhattan, Salina, Dodge City, Garden City, Hays, McPherson, Hutchinson, and Lawrence. Cook PVI: R+16.
whoTheyAre:
Lauren Reinhold is a 55-year-old attorney from Lawrence, Kansas, running as a Democrat for the U.S. House seat in Kansas's 1st Congressional District. She brings 28 years of legal experience to the race, having worked in private practice, as a staff attorney for KU Legal Services for Students, as a managing attorney at an Iowa law firm, and most recently as an attorney-adviser at the Social Security Administration handling disability cases and administrative law. She also served as a federal union officer advocating for government employees.

Reinhold's campaign grew directly out of the DOGE federal workforce reduction effort in early 2026. She was working at the Social Security Administration when the push came for federal employees to resign. Rather than quietly accept the disruption, she channeled that experience into a congressional bid, traveling across the Big First — stopping in Hays, Dodge City, Salina, Hutchinson, and dozens of rural towns — before formally launching her campaign.

She grew up partly in Topeka before her family moved to St. Louis, then returned to Kansas in 1993 to attend KU Law School and stayed to raise a family. She is a volunteer and board member with the Kansas Lawyers Assistance Program, a lawyer wellness organization.
theirRecord:
Reinhold's record is professional rather than legislative. She has never held elected office and has no voting record to examine. Her career spans private law practice, university legal services, and federal government service. Her time at the Social Security Administration gave her direct experience with disability claims and the administrative law process that governs how millions of Americans access federal benefits.

Her campaign platform centers on protecting government services, affordable healthcare, and wages that families can live on. She has framed her candidacy around defending the federal workers and programs that serve ordinary people, and she has been sharply critical of Rep. Tracey Mann, accusing him of prioritizing wealthy interests over the needs of constituents in the district.

Reinhold entered the Democratic primary later than her opponent Colin McRoberts, who filed in September 2025. Her campaign infrastructure is lighter, with fewer visible social media accounts and less press coverage, though she has actively campaigned across the district in person.
whatYouShouldKnow: Reinhold is a lawyer with 28 years of experience who most recently worked as an attorney-adviser at the Social Security Administration, handling disability cases and administrative law matters.
She launched her campaign after the DOGE push to reduce the federal workforce. She accepted the buyout offer from the Social Security Administration and is now running for Congress full-time.
She served as a federal union officer, working in human resources and advocating for federal employees — a role that shapes her worker-centered campaign message.

Both Reinhold and her Democratic primary opponent Colin McRoberts live in Lawrence, the most liberal city in the district. This is widely seen as a vulnerability in a deeply conservative, rural district that has not elected a Democrat since 1952.
She filed for the race months after McRoberts and has less visible campaign infrastructure, including no Twitter, Instagram, or Bluesky presence as of March 2026.
Public records describe her as Christian, but no specific church, denomination, or congregation has been publicly identified or confirmed.
The 1st District carries a Cook PVI of R+16, and Rep. Tracey Mann has won his last three elections with roughly 70% of the vote. The structural math facing any Democratic challenger here is steep.
whereTheyWorship: Public records describe Lauren Reinhold as Christian, but no specific church name, denomination, or congregation has been identified in any available source material. Her campaign website and media appearances do not reference a particular place of worship. Without evidence pointing to a specific church, no congregation can be assigned.

quotes.text: I was a proud public servant. When DOGE pushed hardworking employees like me out the door, I decided if Washington won't fight for people, then I will.
I could run for Congress and try to fight this mess they've made of the federal government. Fight back and restore what the American people need.

It's not about me. It's not about, oh, poor Lauren lost her job to DOGE. That's going to be part of the campaign, but I really care that the American people get the government they deserve, that we get the governmental services we need and deserve to make our lives better, and I feel so passionate about that.
If anyone in Washington wants to push policies that hurt our farmers, our hospitals, our workers, or our towns, they'll have to go through me first.
